changes: Extended `scripts/evals/materialize_semi_real_probe_suite.py` and `tests/test_semi_real_probe_suite.py` so each semi-real probe can pin its own RNG seed, which lets a single manifest hold deterministic anchors for distinct failure modes.

Added `withholding_edge_overlap_bottom_probe` to `evals/semi_real_probes/manifest.json` to stress the applicant/signature area with a bottom-anchored `edge_overlap` variant rather than only top/address-side corruption.
Materialized the new probe, measured its exact/CER/WER baseline, and recorded a discarded applicant-name scoring candidate plus field-group deltas in `evals/augmentation_effects/ledger.json`.

kept: per-probe RNG seed support for deterministic semi-real probe generation
document-level `withholding_edge_overlap_bottom_probe` as a tracked applicant/signature hard case
negative augmentation delta record for `edge_overlap` applicant-name scoring so this failed idea does not get retried blindly
discarded: applicant-name region scoring candidate that preferred a noisy `MARIA L. CHEN` line but regressed probe exact match from 0.8 to 0.7 by emitting `To S. CHEN` and corrupting `first_name`

next_priorities: Inspect applicant-name crop selection with first/last-name anchors or line-level post-filtering that explicitly preserves `MARIA`/`CHEN` before considering any middle-initial rescue.

Prepare a dedicated region-OCR experiment for `applicant_name` versus `middle_name`, because the current `middle_name` template crop is effectively blank on both the original and edge-overlap samples.
